name: Cantrell Grotesk
classification_notes: The name and specimen positioning confirm a grotesque sans classification, with variable weight and width axes and italic variants typical of contemporary grotesque families.
summary: A variable grotesque sans from Cantrell Type spanning a wide weight range with italic support and coverage for 220+ languages.
description: Cantrell Grotesk is a variable-axis grotesque with continuous control over weight, width and italic axes, along with five stylistic sets for additional character alternates. The family covers 484 glyphs and supports over 220 languages across extended Latin scripts. Available under desktop, web and app licensing tiers.
foundry_blurb: Cantrell Type offer Cantrell Grotesk with desktop, web and app licences, noting support for logos, printed materials, physical products, design software, office software, websites, and mobile and desktop apps.

name: Saboneta
classification_notes: The name Saboneta evokes the tradition of Sabon, a classic transitional serif. The presence of swash characters, small caps, oldstyle numerals, and discretionary ligatures reinforces its positioning within the humanist-to-transitional serif tradition.
summary: A serif family with eight weights, swash characters, small caps and oldstyle numerals, covering 220+ languages across 484 glyphs.
description: Saboneta draws on the transitional serif tradition, offering eight weights from Hairline to Black with a full suite of OpenType features including swash alternates, small caps, discretionary ligatures, and a choice of oldstyle or lining numerals. Three stylistic sets allow further customisation of letterforms. Coverage of over 220 languages and 484 glyphs makes it suitable for multilingual editorial and book work.
foundry_blurb: Cantrell Type offer Saboneta with desktop, web and app licensing options. The family covers 220+ supported languages across 484 glyphs, with OpenType features including swash characters, small caps, discretionary ligatures, and oldstyle and lining numerals, alongside three stylistic sets.

name: Thyrus
classification_notes: The presence of swash characters, oldstyle numerals, discretionary ligatures and multiple stylistic sets suggests a refined serif with calligraphic influence, placing it broadly in the transitional or humanist serif category.
summary: A serif with eight weights, swash characters and oldstyle numeral support covering over 220 languages across 484 glyphs.
description: Thyrus is a serif family spanning eight weights from Thin to Black, with OpenType features including swash alternates, discretionary ligatures, three stylistic sets and a choice between default, oldstyle and lining numerals. Its 484-glyph set supports over 220 languages, suggesting broad Latin Extended coverage suited to international editorial and branding work. The interactive specimen on the product page allows weight and tracking adjustment, pointing to versatility across both text and display sizes.
foundry_blurb: Cantrell Type offer Thyrus with desktop, web and app licensing options. The family covers 220+ supported languages across 484 glyphs and includes OpenType features such as swash characters, discretionary ligatures, multiple stylistic sets and oldstyle numerals.

name: Cottonhouse Slab
classification_notes: Described as a slab on the foundry page, with serif characters and swash alternates suggesting a warm, characterful display slab.
summary: A four-weight slab serif with swash characters and ligatures, suited to editorial, branding and printed materials with an americana or heritage feel.
description: A slab serif offered in Regular, Medium, Semibold and Bold, each accompanied by an italic. The family includes swash characters and ligatures alongside a glyph set of 380, giving it enough character for display and branding use. Desktop and digital licences are sold separately, covering logos, printed materials, websites and apps.
foundry_blurb: Cantrell Type offer Cottonhouse Slab with desktop licences covering logos, printed materials, physical products and design software, and separate digital licences for websites, mobile apps, web apps and desktop apps.

name: Staten Sans
classification_notes: The breadth of widths from XXX Compressed to XXX Expanded, the oblique rather than true italic, and the reverse variants point to a grotesque tradition. The variable font axes covering weight, width and italic confirm a contemporary grotesque with broad stylistic range.
summary: A variable grotesque with an unusually wide range of widths, from XXX Compressed to XXX Expanded, across nine weights with oblique and reverse variants.
description: Staten Sans covers an extensive matrix of styles combining nine weights, eight width grades and oblique cuts, with additional reverse variants for display use. The family ships as a variable font with weight, width and italic axes, and 484 glyphs covering over 220 languages. Stylistic sets SS01 through SS05 offer alternate characters for further customisation.
foundry_blurb: Staten Sans is offered by Cantrell Type as a desktop and digital licence. It includes over 484 glyphs, supports 220+ languages, and is available as a variable font with weight, width and italic axes, as well as five stylistic sets for alternate characters.

name: Staten
classification_notes: Staten is a wide-ranging grotesque sans with multiple widths from XXX Compressed to XXX Expanded, reverse cuts, obliques and variable font axes, placing it firmly in the grotesque sans tradition with display ambitions.
summary: A sprawling grotesque sans with widths running from XXX Compressed to XXX Expanded, reverse cuts and a three-axis variable font.
description: Staten covers an unusually broad range of widths, from XXX Compressed through to XXX Expanded, with weights from Hairline to Ultra at each step. Oblique and reverse variants add further range, and the variable font exposes weight, width and italic axes for continuous control. With 484 glyphs and support for over 220 languages, it suits both display and text applications across a wide range of Latin-language projects.
foundry_blurb: Cantrell Type present Staten as a feature-rich grotesque family with an extensive width range, alternate characters via stylistic sets, a variable font with weight, width and italic axes, 484 glyphs, and support for over 220 languages, available under desktop and digital licences.

name: Fitzroy Display
classification_notes: The typeface is explicitly named 'Display' and features swash characters, ligatures, and floral ornaments, pointing clearly to a decorative display serif category.
summary: A display serif with five weights, swash characters, ligatures and floral ornaments across 380 glyphs.
description: Fitzroy Display offers five weights from Light to Bold, accompanied by swash characters, typographic ligatures and floral ornaments. The 380-glyph set suggests a considered range of decorative detail suited to editorial headlines, branding and printed materials. The inclusion of both desktop and digital licensing covers logos, physical products, websites and mobile applications.
foundry_blurb: Cantrell Type offer Fitzroy Display with desktop and digital licensing options covering logos, printed materials, physical products, design software, websites, mobile apps, web apps and desktop apps.

name: Bariloche
classification_notes: The width variants from Condensed through Wide and the overall structure suggest a grotesque sans-serif family with multiple width axes, consistent with a contemporary workhorse grotesque.
summary: A multi-width grotesque sans from Cantrell Type spanning five widths from Condensed to Wide.
description: Bariloche is a grotesque sans-serif offered in five widths: Condensed, Semicondensed, Regular, Expanded and Wide. With 380 glyphs per style, the family covers a reasonable Latin character set for both print and screen applications. Available under separate desktop and digital licensing, it suits logo work, printed materials and digital products.
foundry_blurb: Cantrell Type offer Bariloche across five widths from Condensed to Wide, with 380 glyphs per style. Desktop licences cover logos, printed materials, physical products, design software and office software, while digital licences extend to websites, mobile apps, web apps and desktop apps.

name: Chateau Grande
classification_notes: The width variants and uppercase-focused presentation suggest a display-oriented serif or titling face typical of decorative headline typography; the name and styling imply a classical or ornamental display direction.
summary: A display typeface offered across five widths from Condensed to Expanded, with 380 glyphs and separate desktop and digital licensing.
description: Chateau Grande is a display typeface from Cantrell Type available in five width variants spanning Condensed through Expanded, with weight options of Normal and Semiexpanded. Its 380-glyph set suggests reasonable Latin coverage for headline and branding applications. Licences are split between desktop and digital use, covering logos, print, physical products, websites, apps and office software.
foundry_blurb: Cantrell Type offer Chateau Grande with desktop and digital licences, covering use cases from logos and printed materials to websites and mobile apps. The typeface ships with 380 glyphs and is available across five widths.

name: Kokomo
classification_notes: The page offers no detail beyond a coming-soon notice, so category is inferred from the foundry context; classified as display pending further information.
summary: A forthcoming release from Cantrell Type with no design details yet publicly available.
description: The page is a coming-soon placeholder with no specimen, description or weight information disclosed. No details about the design, intended use or release date are available at this time.
foundry_blurb: Cantrell Type lists Kokomo as a coming-soon release with no accompanying copy or preview imagery on the page.

name: Venetian
classification_notes: The name and foundry page strongly suggest a typeface in the Venetian (humanist oldstyle) serif tradition, characterised by angled stress and bracketed serifs derived from early Renaissance type.
summary: A forthcoming Venetian serif from Cantrell Type, with no release details yet publicly available.
description: The page serves as a holding placeholder for Venetian, a typeface in development by Cantrell Type. No weights, specimens or pricing have been released at this stage. The name indicates a humanist serif drawing on the Venetian oldstyle tradition rooted in fifteenth-century Venetian printing.
foundry_blurb: Cantrell Type lists Venetian as a forthcoming release, with no further details currently provided on the page.

name: Test Fonts
classification_notes: The page does not describe a single typeface but a test font download page; insufficient information to classify accurately, defaulting to sans-serif grotesque based on the mentioned Staten Sans family.
summary: A trial font download page offering limited-character test versions of Staten and Staten Sans for mockup use.
description: Cantrell Type's test font page provides trial versions of Staten and Staten Sans, the two families currently available under their licensing terms. Downloads include both OTF and WOFF2 files with a limited character set suited to print, web and app mockups. No other retail fonts are available as trials at this time due to licensing restrictions.
foundry_blurb: Cantrell Type offer free test fonts for Staten and Staten Sans, noting that licensing agreements restrict trial availability to those two families. The download includes OTF and WOFF2 files with a limited character set, intended for mocking up print, web and app designs.

name: Cantrell Type Licensing
classification_notes: This page contains only licensing information and no specific typeface details; category defaults cannot be accurately determined from the provided content.
summary: A licensing options page for Cantrell Type Foundry covering desktop, digital and test font use cases.
description: This page outlines the licensing structure for all typefaces sold by Cantrell Type Foundry, covering desktop, digital and test font tiers. Licences are one-time fees, never expire and permit worldwide use. No specific typeface details, weights or stylistic information are present on this page.
foundry_blurb: Cantrell Type Foundry offers one-time, non-expiring worldwide licences across desktop, digital and test font tiers, covering uses from print and logos through to web, mobile, broadcast and application embedding.
